title: [RELAX] Fix rotary embedding buffer size calculation
body:
- Change head_dim//2 to rotary_dim//2 in LongRope scaling
- Fixes buffer size when rotary_dim differs from head_dim when partial_rotary_factor < 1.0

step_plan.summary: Fix ext_factors buffer sizing in fused_rope_longrope_scaling by using rotary_dim // 2 instead of head_dim // 2 so the declared buffer length matches the d_range used by rope_freq_longrope and avoids indexing mismatches when rotary_dim differs from head_dim.

steps.operation: Adjust ext_factors T.Buffer declaration in LongRope primfunc
steps.reason: The fused_rope_longrope_scaling T.prim_func currently declares ext_factors with length head_dim // 2, but rope_freq_longrope indexes ext_factors using d % (d_range // 2) where d_range is the rotary_dim passed by the caller. When rotary_dim != head_dim (e.g., partial_rotary_factor < 1.0) this causes buffer size and indexing mismatches. Changing the declaration to T.Buffer((rotary_dim // 2,), ...) aligns the buffer shape with the actual rotation dimension used by LongRope frequency computation.
steps.side_effects: This is a TIR primfunc signature-level change: callers that construct or pass ext_factors must provide a buffer of length rotary_dim // 2 instead of head_dim // 2. Tests covering LongRope and partial_rotary_factor scenarios should be run and updated where callers allocate ext_factors. Downstream code that assumed the old size may break and must be updated; however, the algorithmic math is unchanged so runtime semantics aside from buffer-sizing/indexing should remain the same. Compiled modules or caches that rely on the previous signature may need to be rebuilt.
